title: Ensuring Justice for Victims of Partial-Birth Abortion Act
short_title: Ensuring Justice for Victims of Partial-Birth Abortion Act
plain_summary: This bill tells the Attorney General to look into cases where doctors may have broken the law against partial-birth abortion, including five baby remains that were found in Washington DC in 2022. It makes healthcare workers report when they think this law has been broken. The bill also says the government must write a report every year about how well they are enforcing this law and study how they enforced it in the past.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Ensuring Justice for Victims of Partial-Birth Abortion Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Investigation Into Remains of 5 Babies Discovered in Washington, DC
sections.text: The Attorney General (the top law enforcement officer in the United States) must investigate whether anyone broke the Partial-Birth Abortion Ban Act of two thousand three when five baby remains were found by the DC Metropolitan Police Department on March thirtieth, two thousand twenty-two. The Attorney General must look into whether these deaths violated the federal law that bans a specific type of late-term abortion procedure. Within six months of this law being passed, the Attorney General must give Congress a detailed report about the investigation. The report must describe what happened to each baby and what legal actions, if any, were taken.

sections.title: Section 3  Requirement to Investigate Violations of the Partial-Birth Abortion Ban
sections.text: This section changes the existing federal law about partial-birth abortion. It adds a new requirement that the Attorney General must investigate and take proper legal action when someone is accused of violating the partial-birth abortion ban.

sections.title: Section 4  Mandatory Reporting of Violations
sections.text: Any doctor, nurse, or other healthcare worker must immediately report violations of the partial-birth abortion ban to law enforcement. This also applies to any employee who works at a hospital, doctor's office, or abortion clinic. When these workers know that someone has broken the partial-birth abortion law, they must tell state or federal law enforcement agencies right away, or they can tell both.

sections.title: Section 5  Report on Violations of the Partial-Birth Abortion Ban
sections.text: Every year by October first, the Attorney General must give a report to the House and Senate Judiciary Committees and post it on the Department of Justice website. The report must include several things. First, it must list what steps the Department of Justice took to tell abortion providers about their duty to follow the partial-birth abortion ban. Second, it must say how many times people were accused of breaking this law in the past ten years. Third, it must describe all the efforts the Department made to investigate these accusations. Finally, it must count how many legal actions were taken, including investigations, prosecutions, and criminal charges filed.

sections.title: Section 6  GAO Report
sections.text: Within eighteen months of this law being passed, the Comptroller General of the United States (the head of the Government Accountability Office, which reviews how the government spends money and operates) must study all the legal actions taken for partial-birth abortion violations from fiscal year two thousand four through two thousand twenty-four. The Comptroller General must give a report about this review to the House and Senate Judiciary Committees. The report should include recommendations for making enforcement of the partial-birth abortion ban stronger.

sections.title: Section 7  Severability
sections.text: If any court decides that part of this law is unconstitutional (goes against the Constitution), the rest of the law will still be valid and enforceable. The remaining parts of the law will continue to apply to other people and situations.
